title: Trichophorum oliganthum
taxonomy: Trichophorum oliganthum is a species of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae. It is a type of sedge, commonly found in wetlands and alpine meadows. This plant is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia and the Himalayas. Trichophorum oliganthum has been classified as a perennial herb, characterized by its simple leaves and small, yellowish flowers. Within the plant kingdom, Trichophorum oliganthum is classified as a monocot, belonging to the class Liliopsida.

intro: Trichophorum oliganthum, also known as dwarf cattail or oliganthum, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the Arctic tundra. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of wound healing, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Trichophorum oliganthum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
